dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini benefited from the day's only repeated favorable provider-specific discussion: Gemma 4 was described as fast and useful on a Mac mini, reported to handle a hard coding task correctly, and cited in another thread as a meaningful step up over older small models despite some early tooling rough edges [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini won because Hacker News had direct, product-level praise for Gemma 4 in two separate front-page discussions, while Anthropic's mentions leaned negative and OpenAI and Microsoft Copilot had no meaningful tracked signal. The Gemma comments were not just incidental name-drops: they discussed speed, coding usefulness, and comparative model quality in concrete terms [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude drew direct criticism in two threads: one commenter said a Claude bug was burning through tokens fast enough to push them toward alternatives, and another blamed inaccurate Artemis II trackers on obvious Claude Code usage; the remaining mention mainly treated Claude as the incumbent paid benchmark for dev work [E4][E3][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemma 4 got the clearest concrete praise of the day: commenters said the smaller model was super fast and useful locally, reported correct completion of a difficult coding task, and referenced Gemma 4 as a notable upgrade versus older small models, even with some launch-window tooling caveats [E1][E2].

evidence.summary: In the Gemma 4 Mac mini thread, a commenter said both Gemma 4 variants completed a difficult coding task correctly, called the smaller model 'super speedy,' and said it looked useful for small coding tasks on that hardware.

In the Apfel local-model thread, a commenter contrasted Apple's bundled model with newer options and said Gemma 4 represented a big leap over older small models, preferring the latest model to an older on-device default.
In the Artemis II tracker Show HN, commenters complained that several trackers had major inaccuracies, and a top comment said they 'obviously used Claude Code.'
In the Apfel thread, one commenter said a 'Claude bug' was burning through tokens at record speed, which pushed them to try alternative local models.

In the Gemma 4 setup thread, a commenter described themselves as a heavy Claude user and asked whether local open models were good enough to replace their $20/month Claude subscription for development work.
